Title: A near-complete assembly of asparagus bean provides insights into anthocyanin accumulation in pods

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: PRS; WSS1; Fengchan 6 reference genome context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to purple pod coloration and nutritional quality/appearance traits
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis via regulation of flavonoid/anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Single dominant regulator controlling purple pod trait; key regulator associated with pod anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with upregulation of endogenous structural genes in anthocyanin pathway, including PAL; F3H; DFR; LDOX
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation (inferred from MYB TF role and increased anthocyanin accumulation)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA mapping; differential expression analysis; causal variant identification by Sanger sequencing; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; phenotype observation of transgenic lines; KASP marker co-segregation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and purple pod coloration; CT insertion allele in WSS1 causes loss of function and light green pods
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BSA mapped the purple pod locus to a 0.55 Mb region on chromosome Vu05. 2. Vu05G004180 (VuMYB114) was one of two DEGs in the interval and carries a two-base CT insertion in WSS1 that introduces a premature stop codon. 3. VuMYB114 is homologous to Arabidopsis AtMYB114, an anthocyanin-related R2R3-MYB TF. 4. Overexpression of cVuMYB114 in Arabidopsis produced purple transgenic lines, confirming function. 5. A KASP marker based on the causal variant co-segregated with pod color in the F2 population.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pod color comparison between purple-pod PRS and light-green-pod WSS1 under normal growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher expression in pods of purple-pod cultivar PRS than in light-green-pod WSS1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful causal gene and functional marker target for molecular breeding of purple, anthocyanin-enriched asparagus bean pods; KASP marker VuP-KASP1 can assist selection
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and resequencing/BSA, RNA-seq, metabolomics; sequencing data deposited at NGDC GSA: CRA007957; assembly accession GWHCBIQ00000000
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Purple pod phenotype behaved as a single dominant trait in the F2 population (220 purple : 84 light green). Functional validation was performed in Arabidopsis because asparagus bean transformation is immature.
